name: Docker Extended
description: Extended container protection beyond Docker Baseline: docker/podman prune and force-removal variants, volume and network deletion, and docker compose down -v. Derived from the destructive_command_guard pattern library.

rules.name: Require access request for down volumes
rules.description: docker-compose down -v removes volumes and their data permanently.

rules.name: Require access request for volume prune
rules.description: podman volume prune removes ALL unused volumes and their data permanently.

rules.name: Require justification for system prune
rules.description: docker system prune removes ALL unused containers, networks, images. Use 'docker system df' to preview. Safer alternative: `docker system df -v` — Preview what would be removed without deleting anything.

rules.name: Require justification for volume prune
rules.description: docker volume prune removes ALL unused volumes and their data permanently. Safer alternative: `docker volume ls -q -f dangling=true` — List unused volumes first to review what would be deleted.

rules.name: Require justification for network prune
rules.description: docker network prune removes ALL unused networks. Safer alternative: `docker network ls` — List all networks to review before pruning.

rules.name: Require justification for rm force
rules.description: docker rm -f forcibly removes containers, potentially losing data. Safer alternative: `docker stop {container} && docker rm {container}` — Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM before removal.

rules.name: Require justification for rmi force
rules.description: docker rmi -f forcibly removes images even if in use. Safer alternative: `docker rmi {image}` — Remove without force - fails safely if image is in use.

rules.name: Require justification for volume rm
rules.description: docker volume rm permanently deletes volumes and their data. Safer alternative: `docker volume inspect {volume}` — Inspect volume metadata and mount point before removal.

rules.name: Require justification for stop all
rules.description: Stopping/killing all containers can disrupt services. Be specific about which containers. Safer alternative: `docker stop {container-name}` — Stop specific containers by name.

rules.name: Require justification for down rmi all
rules.description: docker-compose down --rmi all removes all images used by services.

rules.name: Require justification for rm volumes
rules.description: docker-compose rm -v removes volumes attached to containers.

rules.name: Require justification for system prune (2)
rules.description: podman system prune removes ALL unused containers, pods, images. Use 'podman system df' to preview.

rules.name: Warn on image prune
rules.description: docker image prune removes unused images. Use 'docker images' to review first. Safer alternative: `docker images -f dangling=true` — List dangling images first to see what would be removed.

rules.name: Require justification for rm force (2)
rules.description: podman rm -f forcibly removes containers, potentially losing data.

rules.name: Warn on container prune
rules.description: docker container prune removes ALL stopped containers. Safer alternative: `docker ps -a -f status=exited` — List stopped containers first to review before removal.

rules.name: Require justification for rmi force (2)
rules.description: podman rmi -f forcibly removes images even if in use.

rules.name: Warn on rm force
rules.description: docker-compose rm -f forcibly removes containers without confirmation.

rules.name: Require justification for volume rm (2)
rules.description: podman volume rm permanently deletes volumes and their data.

rules.name: Warn on pod prune
rules.description: podman pod prune removes ALL stopped pods.

rules.name: Warn on image prune (2)
rules.description: podman image prune removes unused images. Use 'podman images' to review first.

rules.name: Warn on container prune (2)
rules.description: podman container prune removes ALL stopped containers.
